name: Theory Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: The core model: events enter perception, become behavior, create feedback, and change the next room.
whatItChanges: Helps the Inspector explain why one action can have base, perceived, social, and institutional effects.
inspectorExplanation: This card keeps the game grounded: story is not proof, but consequences can still be studied.

name: Clinical Reality Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Bodies, brains, stress, grief, trauma, sleep, access, and disability can change what feels possible.
whatItChanges: Makes safety, concern, and agent memory pressure easier to read without treating symptoms as clues from the story.
inspectorExplanation: This card explains why the same room is not the same room for every body.

name: Boundary Handrail
plainLanguageMeaning: Reality remains reality. No story, metaphor, theory, or game mechanic outranks safety or consent.
whatItChanges: Keeps story objects from becoming harm rewards. Heavy objects are framed as responsibility, correction, or restraint.
inspectorExplanation: This is the guardrail card. It tells the player what the game refuses to turn into a mechanic.

name: Self Awareness Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: The observer can notice their own pattern before the pattern chooses the next move.
whatItChanges: Supports trigger, loop, and protocol Boulders by making pause, naming, and revision legible.
inspectorExplanation: This card turns self-observation into a playable delay without treating awareness as an instant cure.

name: Geometry as Consequence
plainLanguageMeaning: Repeated relations leave readable shapes: loops, branches, paths, graphs, rooms, and return currents.
whatItChanges: Supports room/path Boulders by explaining why changed architecture changes future behavior.
inspectorExplanation: This card turns layout into meaning without claiming shape proves a hidden message.

name: Media Communication Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Media frames, compresses, repeats, and distributes events until the received version may differ from the sent one.
whatItChanges: Makes signal, echo, and observer-event Boulders raise Witness and Named Weight quickly.
inspectorExplanation: This card explains why attention can make an idea travel farther than its context.

name: Attention Collapse Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: A room loses coherence when people cannot hold evidence long enough to compare it.
whatItChanges: Makes fast echoes more volatile and makes context-preserving cards more important.
inspectorExplanation: This card explains why the game uses short cards: attention needs entry points without flattening the source.

name: Software Systems Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Software is consequence made executable: input becomes validation, state, output, logs, and feedback.
whatItChanges: Makes exported run logs, deterministic tests, and source references part of the fiction-facing mechanics.
inspectorExplanation: This card explains why app data must tell the truth about where it came from.

name: Natural Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Consequence exists before human meaning. Disturbance moves through physical systems whether anyone notices or not.
whatItChanges: Keeps Boulder and weather objects anchored in base reality before interpretation starts.
inspectorExplanation: This card explains why the room can change before anybody has a story for it.

name: Weather Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Atmosphere changes rooms through pressure, light, danger, memory, and mood before humans assign meaning.
whatItChanges: Gives weather-linked Boulders concern pressure without turning storms into signs.
inspectorExplanation: This card carries the simple rule: weather can matter without becoming a command.

name: Power Governance Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Power builds rooms other people have to live inside through law, policy, authority, bureaucracy, and spectacle.
whatItChanges: Makes institutional pressure legible when access, forms, doors, or official language shape the turn.
inspectorExplanation: This card explains why choices can feel unavailable even when an option technically exists.

name: Dream Layer
plainLanguageMeaning: Dreams are simulated rooms. They are not proof or commands, but they can leave waking emotional weather.
whatItChanges: Future dream artifacts can affect mood and concern while staying behind strong safety guardrails.
unlockCondition: Locked: dream artifacts are inspectable later after boundary cards are understood.
inspectorExplanation: This card is visible but intentionally cautious: dreams can affect the day, but they do not control it.
